content: In Isaiah 9:6, 'For to us a child is born.' What does the Hebrew word for 'child' mean?
explanation: Yeled (ילד) means 'child' — referring to Jesus born as an infant.

content: In Isaiah 53:5, 'He was wounded for our transgressions.' What does the Hebrew for 'wounded' mean?
explanation: Chalal (חלל) means 'pierced through, wounded' — pointing to the death of Christ on the cross.

content: Isaiah 1:18: 'Though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be ___'
explanation: The LORD promises full forgiveness, transforming sins as red as blood into white as snow.

content: What did the seraphim cry to one another in Isaiah's vision?
explanation: Isaiah 6:3 — 'Holy' (Kadosh) is repeated three times, emphasizing God's absolute holiness.
options: Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts

content: Isaiah 11:6 describes the peaceful kingdom: 'The wolf shall dwell with ___'
explanation: Isaiah 11:6 — A vision of the peaceful age when Messiah reigns, all hostility is reconciled.

content: Isaiah 40:8: 'The grass withers, the flower fades, but the ___ of our God will stand forever'
explanation: Everything on earth is temporary, but God's Word is eternal. 1 Peter 1:24-25 quotes this verse.

content: How does Isaiah 55:8-9 compare God's thoughts and ways to man's?
explanation: God's thoughts are higher than man's as the heavens are higher than the earth. We cannot fully comprehend Him.
options: As high above as the heavens are above the earth

content: Isaiah 64:8 compares God and us to what?
explanation: God is the potter, we are the clay. He has the right to shape us as He wills.

content: When the LORD asked, 'Whom shall I send?' how did Isaiah reply?
explanation: Isaiah 6:8 — 'Here am I! Send me.' Isaiah was ready to obey after being cleansed.

content: Isaiah 7:14 prophesies: 'Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and shall call his name ___'
explanation: Isaiah 7:14 — Immanuel (God with us). This prophecy is fulfilled in Jesus (Matt 1:23).

content: By what names does Isaiah 9:6 call the coming Messiah?
explanation: Isaiah 9:6 — Four titles showing Jesus is both God and man, both King and Priest.
options: Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace

content: What does Isaiah 40:31 say will happen to those who wait for the LORD?
explanation: Isaiah 40:31 — Those who wait for the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles, run and not be weary, walk and not faint.
options: Renew strength, mount up with wings like eagles

content: To what animal does Isaiah 53:6 compare humanity?
explanation: Isaiah 53:6 — All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way. But the LORD has laid on Him (Christ) the iniquity of us all.

content: Isaiah 53:7 describes the Suffering One 'like a lamb that is led to the slaughter.' To what event does this image point?
explanation: Isaiah 53:7 — Jesus stood silent before Pilate (Matt 27:12-14), like a lamb led to slaughter. Philip used this passage to preach the gospel to the eunuch (Acts 8:32-35).
options: Jesus silent before Pilate, then crucified

content: Whom does Isaiah 14:12 describe as fallen from heaven like the morning star?
explanation: Isaiah 14:12 — 'How you are fallen from heaven, O Day Star, son of Dawn! How you are cut down to the ground, you who laid the nations low!' Traditionally understood as Lucifer/Satan.
options: The Day Star (Lucifer) — applied to Satan

content: Whom does Isaiah 26:3 say God keeps in perfect peace?
explanation: Isaiah 26:3 — 'You keep him in perfect peace whose mind is stayed on you, because he trusts in you.'
options: The one whose mind is stayed on Him

content: What does Isaiah 41:10 promise to God's people?
explanation: Isaiah 41:10 — 'Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you.'
options: Fear not — I am with you and will strengthen you

content: What does the LORD do with His people's sins, according to Isaiah 43:25?
explanation: Isaiah 43:25 — 'I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.'
options: Blot them out and remember them no more
Pass them on to the descendants

content: Whom does Isaiah 61:1-2 describe as anointed to bring good news to the poor?
explanation: Isaiah 61:1-2 — 'The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me, because the LORD has anointed me to bring good news to the poor.' Jesus applied this to Himself (Luke 4:18-19).
options: The Messiah (Jesus quoted it of Himself)

content: What does Isaiah 65:17 promise God will create?
explanation: Isaiah 65:17 — 'For behold, I create new heavens and a new earth, and the former things shall not be remembered or come into mind.'
options: New heavens and a new earth
